{"credits": [], "cve_id": "CVE-2026-35167", "cvss": {"score": 7.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_severities": {"cvss_v3": {"score": 7.1, "vector_string": "CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:L/PR:L/UI:N/S:U/C:H/I:L/A:N"}, "cvss_v4": {"score": 0.0, "vector_string": null}}, "cwes": [{"cwe_id": "CWE-22", "name": "Improper Limitation of a Pathname to a Restricted Directory ('Path Traversal')"}], "description": "### Impact
The `_get_versioned_path()` method in kedro/io/core.py constructs filesystem paths by directly interpolating user-supplied version strings without sanitization. Because version strings are used as path components, traversal sequences such as ../ are preserved and can escape the intended versioned dataset directory.
This is reachable through multiple entry points: `catalog.load(..., version=...)`, `DataCatalog.from_config(..., load_versions=...)`, and the CLI via `kedro run --load-versions=dataset:../../../secrets`. An attacker who can influence the version string can force Kedro to load files from outside the intended version directory, enabling unauthorized file reads, data poisoning, or cross-tenant data access in shared environments.

### Patches
Yes. Fixed in kedro version 1.3.0. Users should upgrade to kedro >= 1.3.0.

### Workarounds
Validate version strings before passing them to DataCatalog or the CLI, ensuring they do not contain `..` segments, path separators, or absolute paths.", "epss": {"percentage": 0.00327, "percentile": 0.24562}, "ghsa_id": "GHSA-6326-w46w-ppjw", "github_reviewed_at": "2026-04-03T03:46:48Z", "html_url": "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6326-w46w-ppjw", "identifiers": [{"type": "GHSA", "value": "GHSA-6326-w46w-ppjw"}, {"type": "CVE", "value": "CVE-2026-35167"}], "nvd_published_at": "2026-04-06T18:16:43Z", "published_at": "2026-04-03T03:46:48Z", "references": ["https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/security/advisories/GHSA-6326-w46w-ppjw", "https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro/pull/5442", "https://nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-35167", "https://github.com/pypa/advisory-database/tree/main/vulns/kedro/PYSEC-2026-71.yaml", "https://github.com/advisories/GHSA-6326-w46w-ppjw"], "repository_advisory_url": "https://api.github.com/repos/kedro-org/kedro/security-advisories/GHSA-6326-w46w-ppjw", "severity": "high", "source_code_location": "https://github.com/kedro-org/kedro", "summary": "Kedro: Path Traversal in versioned dataset loading via unsanitized version string", "type": "reviewed", "updated_at": "2026-06-06T00:23:00Z", "url": "https://api.github.com/advisories/GHSA-6326-w46w-ppjw", "vulnerabilities": [{"first_patched_version": "1.3.0", "package": {"ecosystem": "pip", "name": "kedro"}, "vulnerable_functions": [], "vulnerable_version_range": "< 1.3.0"}], "withdrawn_at": null}

reasons: 資産不一致または判定情報不足

title: Kedro: Path Traversal in versioned dataset loading via unsanitized version string
